designSummary: Architectural diagnosis for sidepanel message duplication: duplication persists during streaming and after completion, and survives refresh/reopen, so the defect crosses live SSE ingestion, background state synchronization, persistence, and transcript projection boundaries. Existing brownfield fit is retained in extension/src/sidepanel/**, extension/src/background/**, and extension/src/shared/**. The design decision is to harden upstream canonical event acceptance and observability rather than relying on late transcript heuristics. Stream consumption must accept only canonical ordered events, log accepted/rejected/gap/out-of-order cases, and synchronize only reconciled state. Transcript projection must consume accepted canonical events, preserve stable logical message identity and deterministic order, and emit projection anomaly diagnostics. Background synchronization must reconcile run-state snapshots using origin/frontier/version rules so stale or replayed sidepanel snapshots do not reintroduce duplicate events after refresh or reopen.
designDecisions: ADR: Preserve existing brownfield module ownership. Extend ELM-COMP-EXT-SIDEPANEL (extension/src/sidepanel/useSidepanelController.ts, model.ts, reasoningTimeline.ts), ELM-COMP-EXT-BACKGROUND (extension/src/background/index.ts), and ELM-COMP-EXT-SHARED (extension/src/shared/api.ts, history.ts, shared types/protocol) instead of introducing a new physical package. These modules already own stream ingestion, transcript projection, state sync, SSE contract parsing, and durable history persistence, so they are the cleanest software-unit boundaries for the correction.

ADR: Dedupe authority moves upstream. Duplicate prevention must occur at canonical event acceptance in the sidepanel stream consumer and at background reconciliation, not only through transcript text coalescing. Text-similarity compaction remains presentation-only and must never be the primary correctness rule for event identity or order.

ADR: Observability is a required architectural behavior for this fix. Diagnostic logging must correlate runId, raw event id, canonical event key, semantic identity, sequence, accepted/rejected decision, frontier movement, persistence write result, background sync snapshot version/origin, and transcript anomaly classification so the team can distinguish SSE duplication from projection or re-sync duplication.

issues.details: Blocking audit gap for commit 743a04e5499593851dd6f76170e02c5cb55b2a80. Intended IDs from TASK-001..TASK-004 are ELM-FUNC-EXT-CONSUME-RUN-STREAM, ELM-FUNC-EXT-PROJECT-TRANSCRIPT, ELM-FUNC-EXT-RECONCILE-RUN-STATE, and ELM-FUNC-EXT-CALL-ADAPTER-API. run_reality_scanner verified only requirement-level test tagging and did not verify these architecture IDs in automated tests. This is an IntentNotVerified gap; QA must add tagged automated tests before release can continue.

issues.summary: QA gap: missing architecture-traceable tests for sidepanel duplicate-message fix

Direct automated tests for canonical sidepanel event acceptance and diagnostics in extension/src/sidepanel/model.ts were not found. Add targeted tagged tests covering duplicate, stale replay, out-of-order, gap, transcript anomaly, background reconciliation, and idempotent persistence behaviors, then rerun local verification.

release.summary: Release finalized for full-model issue fix addressing severe sidepanel AI message duplication across streaming, completion, and refresh/reopen paths. Release scope commit: 016599612be0527d5affcdfb083d14ee8bc70eed. Runtime tasks completed: TASK-001, TASK-002, TASK-003, TASK-004. QA status: passed. Audit status: passed. Release log records mandatory traceability matrix artifact for commit 016599612be0527d5affcdfb083d14ee8bc70eed and notes that generated graph-backed verification still shows Requirement and Source Files as N/A with rows marked ❌ No, so documentation retains traceability gap notes despite successful QA/audit.
